# UC-17.1.60 · ISE on AWS / Azure / GCP — Cloud-Hosted PSN Health and Egress Cost

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*If ISE is running in the cloud — Amazon, Microsoft, Google — we watch the rented computer's busyness AND how much data it sends out. Sending too much data outbound means a big bill or a misconfigured setup.*

---

## Description

Detects cloud-hosted ISE PSN resource saturation (CPU >70%) and unusual egress traffic (>1 GB/period) that may indicate misconfigured RADIUS proxying or data exfiltration.

## Value

Cloud-hosted ISE PSNs introduce egress-cost risk. A misconfigured remote-site RADIUS pointing all auth traffic at a cloud PSN multiplies the cloud egress bill. Catching the egress anomaly early prevents bill surprise.

## Implementation

Onboard cloud TAs. Tag ISE EC2/VM instances with `ise=true`. Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger high CPU on a lab cloud PSN. UC-17.1.60 fires.

### Negative scenario

CPU returns to normal. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cloud TAs deployed.
- Instance tagging convention.

### Step 1 — Configure cloud TA inputs.
### Step 2 — Search.
### Step 3 — Validate.
### Step 4 — Dashboard with cost projection.
### Step 5 — Troubleshooting: instance sizing; egress optimisation via VPC peering vs internet egress.

## SPL

```spl
(index=cloud sourcetype IN ("aws:cloudwatch", "mscs:metrics", "google:gcp:metrics") metric_name IN ("CPUUtilization", "NetworkOut") instance_tag="ise")
| stats avg(value) as avg max(value) as max by metric_name, instance_id
| where (metric_name="CPUUtilization" AND avg > 70) OR (metric_name="NetworkOut" AND max > 1e9)
```

## Visualization

(1) Per-instance CPU/Network timechart. (2) Egress trend over 7d. (3) Top-N by egress.

## Known False Positives

- **Initial deployment** — bootstrap traffic.
- **CRL/OCSP refresh** — periodic increased egress.
